story_title: When Everything Changed: A Medical Crisis Story

common_themes.description: Life changes in seconds - no warning, no preparation
examples: Andy's blowtorch pain on conference call

common_themes.description: Who am I when my body/mind fails?
examples: Celeste not knowing what a President is
Andy thinking 'this isn't the man you married'

common_themes.description: Unable to speak, be understood, or understand
Loved ones provide reason to fight and practical support
Unexpected help from people you don't know
Accepting new reality, new self, new limitations

examples: Andy throwing tennis ball hand to hand

top_emotional_moments.description: Trying to call for help, colleague sounds like 'Woo woo woo' - then realizes SHE sounds like a Golden Retriever too
top_emotional_moments.esl_parallel: Trying to communicate in new language, being misunderstood

top_emotional_moments.description: At rock bottom crying uncontrollably, son holds her and says 'You will be fine, Mum, because you have me'
Father diagnosed with leukemia 1 week after Andy's stroke discharge, dies in May. Andy's single recovery goal: get strong enough to carry the coffin
Saw pelican on shore with wings outstretched, stood behind it, held out her own arms, felt 'greatest sense of freedom wash over me'
Turkish Muslim family couldn't remove headscarves in public, so demonstrated tying techniques using serviettes. Two weeks later drove hours to bring her scarves and teach her
Woke from car accident not knowing who she was, didn't recognize son, asked 'Who was the President?' then realized she didn't even know what a President IS
top_emotional_moments.esl_parallel: Starting from zero in new country/language

top_emotional_moments.description: Brain chatter goes silent, feels enormous and expansive, 'like a genie liberated from her bottle,' finds Nirvana while dying
Couldn't walk, had to crawl upstairs to bed while thinking it was just vertigo
top_emotional_moments.esl_parallel: Body failing when mind hasn't caught up

top_emotional_moments.description: Brother reminds him of Kilimanjaro guides' advice: go 'pole pole' - slowly, slowly, small steps. They reached the summit that way.
top_emotional_moments.esl_parallel: Language learning requires small consistent steps

top_emotional_moments.description: Couldn't remember who she was before, so decided to CHOOSE who to become. Created 'happiness project' - chose to be known as a happy person
top_emotional_moments.esl_parallel: Immigrants can choose who they become in new country

esl_resonance_multipliers.reason: Jill's Golden Retriever moment = exact feeling of ESL learner trying to be understood
Celeste's amnesia = starting over with no past, like immigration
Universal anchor - Janine's son, Andy's wife, Anne's husband
Medical system navigation = bureaucratic navigation in new country
Anne's Turkish family = strangers helping immigrants

act_1_normal_life.description: Protagonist living full life - career, family, health

act_2_crisis_hits.description: Sudden onset - no warning, world shatters

act_3_darkest_moment.description: Rock bottom - loss of identity, abilities, hope

act_4_turning_point.description: Small hope appears - person, moment, realization

act_5_small_steps.description: Slow recovery through repetition, patience, support

act_6_transformation.description: New normal, new purpose, helping others

composite_story_recommendations.protagonist_profile: Professional in their 40s, healthy, active, family - could be any gender/background
composite_story_recommendations.setting: Home when crisis hits, hospital, rehabilitation, return to life
key_scenes_to_include: Family member recognizing signs, calling for help
Family member's words as turning point
First small victory (squeeze fingers, walk to bench)
quotable_lines_to_adapt: I became an infant in an adult's body
The room was spinning like I was drunk
You will be fine, because you have me
I decided to sculpt a new personality
Bald me is normal me. Tired me is normal me.
Life has to give up on me - I'm not giving up on life
